name: YouTube Kids
description: Google's video streaming service for children with curated content, parental controls, and age-appropriate filtering.

years.notes: Launch wordmark (Feb 2015 – 2017). Reusing the contemporary YouTube 2013-2017 play-button mark (red rounded-rectangle 'tube' with gradient and white triangle) as the era-equivalent standalone icon — the YouTube Kids 2015 wordmark embeds this same red 'Tube' container, but library convention (matching simple-icons, font-awesome) is to use the standalone play-button at icon scale. Path data sourced from the YouTube 2013 icon (placed centred in the 24×24 viewBox with 3.55 unit vertical padding).

Square 'wonky' play-button icon introduced with the 2018-2019 rebrand, used through 2024. Hand-drawn dark grey (#282828) outline, classic YouTube red (#FF0000) body, white triangle, pink/coral (#FF8983) highlight on top. Native artwork is 833×673.7; placed centred in the 24×24 viewBox with 2.296 unit vertical padding.

Q4 2024 rebrand (Oct 22, 2024) aligning with the global YouTube refresh: pinker red (#FF0033) replacing the previous #FF0000, pure black (#000000) outline replacing #282828, and the pink/coral highlight on top has been dropped. Geometry kept from the 2019 mark; only fills and the highlight layer changed. Sourced from the App Store iTunes Lookup API (1024×1024 PNG); filename in the URL ('logo_youtube_kids_2024_q4_color') confirms the millesime.

notes: Parent relationship dropped — meta.json schema enforces single-level parents and 'youtube' already has parent 'google'. The accurate product hierarchy would be youtube-kids → youtube → google; documenting it here since the manifest can only express one level.
